_doc: Per-room (per-SCENE) access logic for the Ys Origin apworld.
Region identity is the SCENE id (e.g. S_1004), because scene->room and
scene->zone are clean functions while room NAMES repeat across zones
(Boss Room / Save Point / Staircase). current_scene (+0x36C100, g_flags
[0x1F9]) is what the game exposes live, so scene is also the detection key.
A scene listed under `scenes` is AUTHORED: it gets EXACTLY the incoming
edges in its `from` list and its default (permissive, zone-gated) edge is
suppressed. A scene NOT listed stays on the default edge: reachable as soon
as its zone is (the coarse boss-medallion backstop), so un-authored zones
remain beatable. Author zone-by-zone (Wailing Blue first); every
location-bearing scene in a zone must be reachable through authored edges
before that zone is considered authored.
`from` is a list of [source, requirement] edges. `source` is another scene
id (S_xxxx) OR a zone name (the zone-region entry, e.g. 'Wailing Blue').
`requirement` is a list treated as AND of terms; a term that is itself a
list is an OR-group. Item/skill names must match items.json (e.g. the wind
skill is 'Ventus Bracelet'). Examples:
[]                                  -> no requirement (free traversal)
['Ventus Bracelet']                 -> needs the wind skill
['Bronze Key', ['Ventus Bracelet','Dash']] -> Bronze Key AND (wind OR dash)
Part-3 tooling (tools/scenelog.py during a vanilla run) auto-builds the
adjacency + the obtained-before-each-room inventory to seed this file.
